phrases.intent_en: Ask where Marie Antoinette's cell is preserved
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve la cellule reconstituée de Marie-Antoinette ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah seh-LOOL ruh-kohn-stee-TWAY duh mah-REE-ahn-twah-NET
phrases.cultural_note: Marie Antoinette was imprisoned at the Conciergerie from August to October 1793 before her execution. Her cell has been reconstructed as a chapel by Louis XVIII in her memory. A lock of her hair and a crucifix are among the objects on display.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the medieval royal palace history
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous m'expliquer l'histoire du palais royal médiéval ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY voo mex-plee-KAY lee-STWAR doo pah-LEH rwah-YAL may-dee-ay-VAL
phrases.cultural_note: The Conciergerie was built as a royal palace in the 10th century and was the seat of French government until Charles V moved to the Louvre in 1358. It then became a prison, notorious during the Revolution as the last stop before the guillotine.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how many people were imprisoned here during the Revolution
phrases.text_local: Combien de prisonniers ont été détenus ici pendant la Révolution ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohm-BYEHN duh pree-zoh-NYAY ohn ay-TAY day-tuh-NOO ee-SEE pahn-DAHN lah ray-voh-LOO-syohn
phrases.cultural_note: During the Reign of Terror (1793-94), over 2,700 prisoners passed through the Conciergerie before being guillotined on the Place de la Révolution (now Place de la Concorde). Among them: Marie Antoinette, Robespierre, and the poet André Chénier.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a combined ticket with Sainte-Chapelle is available
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un billet combiné avec la Sainte-Chapelle ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL uhn bee-YEH kohm-bee-NAY ah-VEK lah seht shah-PEL
phrases.cultural_note: A combined ticket covering both the Conciergerie and Sainte-Chapelle is available and offers good value. Both sites are managed by the Centre des monuments nationaux and are adjacent to each other on the Île de la Cité.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the medieval kitchens are
phrases.text_local: Où se trouvent les cuisines médiévales ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lay kwee-ZEEN may-dee-ay-VAL
phrases.cultural_note: The four medieval towers and the vast Gothic kitchen hall are among the best-preserved medieval interiors in France. The kitchen could prepare meals for up to 2,000 people at the royal court — it features four enormous fireplaces.
